message:
runtime_compat: inherit ambient cx in Mutex::lock fallback

Same orphan-cx pattern fixed in WorkflowContext::send_* (02a599a9) and
WorkflowRunner fallbacks (09014aae). Mutex::lock, the foundational
wrapper around asupersync::sync::Mutex::lock, built its fallback cx
via crate::cx::for_request() — discarding any ambient Cx::current() the
caller already held. The doc on lock_with_cx at line 238 even names
Cx::current as the lookup the cx-first sibling avoids, implying the
legacy wrapper was meant to honour it.

Switch Mutex::lock to the ambient-inheriting idiom:

    let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

so callers that hold an ambient cx but invoke the legacy .lock() (rather
than .lock_with_cx) now propagate cancellation through the acquire.
Behaviour unchanged when no ambient cx is present. Four sibling
primitives in the same file (RwLock::read/write, Semaphore::acquire,
Semaphore::acquire_owned at lines 310/339/497/549) carry the same bug
and should follow in a sibling commit; scoping this one to the single
site per review instructions.

cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --offline on /tmp/ft-pane1-target
passes clean in 1m25s. (--no-default-features is pre-existingly broken
at HEAD with 61 E0433 errors for `crate::cx::Cx` references outside the
feature gate in wezterm.rs et al., independent of this change.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
